title: Lantana bazeiana
taxonomy: Lantana bazeiana is a species within the genus Lantana and the family Verbenaceae. The genus Lantana is a part of the order Lamiales. This species is known to be a member of the subfamily Verbenoideae. Lantana bazeiana is a flowering plant that is native to South America. The exact classification within the Verbenaceae family is not specified.
uses: Lantana bazeiana, a lesser-known species of the Lantana genus, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and flowers are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for wounds and skin infections. The plant is also thought to possess antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. In some traditional medicine practices, Lantana bazeiana is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.

intro: Lantana bazeiana, also known as yellow lantana, is a species of flowering plant in the verbena family, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers in shades of yellow and orange. The leaves and flowers of Lantana bazeiana have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include lantanoic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal and therapeutic effects. Lantana bazeiana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to aid in the treatment of fever, coughs, and respiratory infections.
